methodology.unit: the actual first five production-index results
methodology.pass_rule: At least four of five are stylistically coherent and positions 1-3 contain no obvious wrong-scene item.
methodology.automatic_failures: Any duplicate original, seed-title variant, slowed/reverb, karaoke, tribute, cover, or mashup in the first five fails the seed.
methodology.scope: Recording names and artists were inspected. This is a transparent ranked-list judgment, not a claim that every full recording was listened to end-to-end.
methodology.blindness: The pair target rank and method-level aggregate score were not used to assign coherence.
methodology.role: Secondary direct UX guardrail only; these judgments are never blended into the pure-sonic pair-retrieval primary.
methodology.winner_top5_provenance: The stable sonic policy preserves the previously judged guarded top five exactly, so no labels were changed or reinterpreted.

judgments.baseline_reason: Five hip-hop/trap artists; no wrong-scene top-three item.
judgments.winner_reason: Five hip-hop/trap artists; no prohibited derivative.

judgments.baseline_reason: Rap and emo-rap neighbors throughout the list.
judgments.winner_reason: Rap and emo-rap neighbors throughout the list.

judgments.baseline_reason: The first two results are hip-hop/drill rather than funk-pop/R&B and the list is scattered.
judgments.winner_reason: Jordan Rakei, Gabriel Jacoby, Thundercat and Amp Fiddler form a coherent funk/R&B list; DJ Quik is the one adjacent outlier.

judgments.baseline_reason: French rap is first and the top three drift through rap and Afrobeats rather than melodic pop.
judgments.winner_reason: The first four are melodic pop/R&B artists; the fifth is the only stylistic stretch.

judgments.baseline_reason: Orchestral and hard-rock items occupy positions two and three.
judgments.winner_reason: The top three are mainstream pop; the two rock/electropop items remain adjacent rather than scene-breaking.

judgments.baseline_reason: Pop-rock/pop-punk list with no prohibited derivative.
judgments.winner_reason: Pop-rock dominates positions one through three and the remaining pop items are compatible.

judgments.baseline_reason: Garage, punk and alternative-rock neighbors throughout.
judgments.winner_reason: Garage, punk and alternative-rock neighbors throughout; the live Ramones item is not the seed recording.

judgments.baseline_reason: A game soundtrack and hardcore punk occupy positions one and two before the first shoegaze result.
judgments.winner_reason: Swirlies, My Bloody Valentine and Cocteau Twins lead a shoegaze/dream-pop list; the two remaining artists are adjacent alternative rock.

judgments.baseline_reason: Ashanti is first, the list crosses unrelated pop/math-rock scenes, and a sped-up derivative appears fifth.
judgments.winner_reason: The sped-up derivative is gone, but Ashanti remains at position two and fewer than four items are clearly hyperpop-adjacent.

judgments.seed: Harder, Better, Faster, Stronger — Daft Punk
judgments.baseline_reason: Blues, reggae and rap occupy the first four positions.
judgments.winner_reason: The top five still cross reggae, indie rock and blues; the guarded pool lacks enough French-house candidates.

judgments.baseline_reason: Five heavy/alternative-metal results including the sourced counterpart.
judgments.winner_reason: Five heavy/alternative-metal results; the counterpart improves from rank five to four.

judgments.baseline_reason: Although the scene is jazz, position one is the same composition under the one-character misspelling Orinthology, which is a prohibited seed-title variant.
judgments.winner_reason: The seed-title variant is removed and all five remaining results are jazz.

judgments.baseline_reason: Four jazz/funk players precede the only clear city-pop artist at rank five.
judgments.winner_reason: Momoko Kikuchi and Anri lead; the remaining jazz-funk artists reflect city-pop's production vocabulary without a wrong-scene top-three result.

judgments.seed: Waka Waka (This Time for Africa) — Shakira
judgments.baseline_reason: Latin, Afro-house and Afrobeats dominate the top three.
judgments.winner_reason: Latin, Brazilian funk and Afro-house dominate the top three; no prohibited derivative.

judgments.baseline_reason: Pop/R&B ballads and vocal pop are coherent for the seed.
judgments.winner_reason: Pop/R&B vocalists occupy the top three; gospel at four is adjacent to the ballad/gospel character.

judgments.baseline_reason: Afrobeats/dancehall artists occupy positions one through three.
judgments.winner_reason: Afrobeats/dancehall artists occupy all five positions.

judgments.baseline_reason: The deployed resolver selects Treasure (Sharam Club Remix), not the requested original recording, so this automatically fails despite a coherent house list.
judgments.winner_reason: A title/artist collision promotes the K-pop group TREASURE to position one and the list remains mixed.

judgments.seed: Destruction of a Statue — Suicide Silence
judgments.baseline_reason: Deathcore/extreme-metal throughout.
judgments.winner_reason: Deathcore/extreme-metal throughout.

judgments.baseline_reason: Alternative/indie-rock list; no wrong-scene top-three item.
judgments.winner_reason: Alternative/indie-rock list led by Paramore; the sourced counterpart remains frozen at rank 45.

judgments.baseline_reason: Reggaetón/Latin-rap throughout.
judgments.winner_reason: Reggaetón/Latin-rap throughout.
